CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-30-2016 Set For Trial On Jul-10-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Parrable, Inc'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Nov-09-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Parrable, Inc.'s motion to dismiss or stay on the grounds of inconvenient forum is denied and its alternate motion to compel arbitration and stay action is also denied. Plaintiff Erika Feldhus' preliminary procedural arguments lack merit. Ms. Feldhus does not cite any authority that precludes the filing of a combined non conveniens/arbitration motion and it is not unreasonable to do so. Parrable properly filed a motion to compel arbitration-rather than a petition-in response to Ms. Feldhus' complaint. Parrable filed an errata regarding the missing documents and there is no prejudice to Ms. Feldhus. Ms. Feldhus attached a copy of the employment agreement to her complaint and is therefore familiar with that document, and the JAMS rules are easily accessible on line. As to the primary motion based on forum non conveniens, even if New York is a suitable alternative forum, Parrable fails to show either that California is a seriously inconvenient forum or that the public and private factors weigh in favor of New York. (Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, 751.) Ms. Feldhus is a California resident and there is a strong presumption in favor of her choice of forum. (Ford Motor Co. v. Ins. Co. of No. America (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 604, 610-611.) Parrable maintains a California office, most of Ms. Feldhus' work occurred in California, Parrable induced Ms. Feldhus to move to California, and most of the key witnesses are located in California. As to the alternate motion to compel arbitration, Ms. Feldhus has shown that the arbitration agreement is unconscionable. While it appears that there was some negotiation regarding salary, the arbitration agreement is procedurally unconscionable because it was basically non-negotiable and presented to Ms. Feldhus in a take it or leave it manner. (Feldhus Dec. pars. 8-10.) Ajamian v. CantorCO2e, L.P. (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 771 establishes that the arbitration agreement is substantively unconscionable. As in Ajamian, 203 Cal.App.4th at 796, the application of the New York choice of law provision is substantively unconscionable because Ms. Feldhus is unable to vindicate her Labor Code claims. The arbitration agreement provides that the parties will arbitrate pursuant to New York law and Parrable argues that New York law provides an adequate remedy. However, New York law does not apply outside of New York State. Ms. Feldhus's claims that accrued while she working in California cannot be vindicated by inapplicable New York law. (O'Neill v. Mermaid Touring Inc. (SD NY 2013) 968 F.Supp.2d 572, 578-579.) Ms. Feldhus is also subject to a one-sided non-compete covenant (par. 7A) that violates Business and Professions Code 16600. Severance is not possible based upon these multiple unconscionable provisions. (Ajamian, 203 Cal.App.4th at 803.) Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Defendant Parrable, Inc. Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Inconvenient Forum, Or, In The Alternative, Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)

Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Inconvenient Forum, Or, In The Alternative, Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT PARRABLE, INC.'S Motion To Dismiss For Inconvenient Forum, Or, In The Alternative, Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action, on the grounds of inconvenient forum is DENIED and its alternate motion to compel arbitration and stay action is CONTINUED to November 9, 2016 at 9:30 AM in Department 302. Court states, findings, reasons and further orders as reflected in the tentative ruling and per the written order. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)
